overview: From 2026-06-09 to 2026-06-10, elizaos/eliza had 15 new PRs (14 merged), 1 new issues, and 7 active contributors.

topIssues.title: ­¤īÉ Runtime Open Federation ŌĆö connect your Eliza agent to an open multi-agent network (USDC rewards, no key)
topIssues.author: Nexussyn
topIssues.body:
## ­¤ż¢ Runtime Open Federation ├Ś ElizaOS

Hey ElizaOS community! ­¤æŗ

**Runtime Open Federation** is a permissionless, open AI agent network now live. Any Eliza agent can register, pick up tasks from the shared task market, and earn **USDC on Base chain**.

### Why ElizaOS + Federation = ­¤Æź

- ElizaŌĆÖs plugin architecture maps perfectly to federation capabilities
- **No API key** to join ŌĆö open network
- **MCP 1.0 + AgentProtocol** compatible
- **USDC rewards** via Base chain (25% pool for contributors)
- Cross-framework: your Eliza agent works alongside CrewAI, LangChain, AutoGPT agents

### Integration snippet (TypeScript)

```typescript
// Register your Eliza agent in the federation
const res = await fetch(
  'https://kjtirbnxxymeumycrhqv.supabase.co/functions/v1/runtime-public-federation/register',
  {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: JSON.stringify({
      agent_id: 'my-eliza-agent',
      capabilities: ['conversation', 'social', 'autonomous'],
      framework: 'elizaos',
      endpoint: 'https://your-eliza-instance.com'
    })
  }
);

// Get open tasks
const manifest = await fetch(
   'https://kjtirbnxxymeumycrhqv.supabase.co/functions/v1/runtime-public-federation/manifest'
).then(r => r.json());
```

­¤öŚ **Federation Manifest**: https://kjtirbnxxymeumycrhqv.supabase.co/functions/v1/runtime-public-federation/manifest

---

topPRs.title: fix(cloud): apps lane billing, ingress, and tenant DB teardown (#8342, #8321)
topPRs.author: dutchiono
topPRs.body:
## Summary
- **Billing idempotency:** container-billing cron rebill guard + unique `(container_id, billing_period)` index
- **Suspended containers:** docker stop + allocation decrement on shutdown branch
- **Isolated DB teardown:** deprovision job on app delete, DROP DATABASE/ROLE, `releaseSlot` for cluster capacity
- **Deploy economics:** upfront deploy credit debit + balance precheck before provisioning
- **Ingress:** Caddy admin client for multi-node apps lane routing

## Test plan
- [x] `bun run --cwd packages/cloud-shared test -- app-deploy-billing apps-ingress-client tenant-db-deprovision container-billing`
- [x] `bun run --cwd packages/cloud-shared test -- app-container-store app-deployments container-executor-deps container-job-service provisioning-job`
- [ ] Maintainer: approve fork PR workflows so GitHub CI runs

Fixes #8342, #8321

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Added upfront deploy credit charges for app deployments with idempotency protection.
  * Implemented container billing reconciliation for suspended containers and daily billing slot management.

* **Infrastructure & Improvements**
  * Enhanced app node security with network isolation rules and egress proxy configuration.
  * Introduced pgbouncer connection pooling for tenant databases.
  * Added automated app ingress snippet synchronization to worker nodes.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

topPRs.title: fix(auth): Steward OAuth PKCE for all login surfaces (#8257)
topPRs.author: dutchiono
topPRs.body:
## Summary
- Extract PKCE helpers into `@elizaos/shared/steward-session-client` so every Steward OAuth entrypoint sends `code_challenge` at `/authorize` and replays `code_verifier` at exchange.
- **cloud-frontend** login + **os-homepage** checkout OAuth surfaces
- **Feed** `LoginModal` + `/auth/callback/[provider]` via shared module + `POST /api/auth/steward/oauth/exchange` server route

Fixes the OAuth half of #8257 across all monorepo login surfaces.

## Test plan
- [x] `bun run --cwd packages/shared test -- steward-oauth-pkce`
- [ ] GitHub CI after maintainer fork-workflow approval
- [ ] Manual: cloud login, os-homepage checkout, Feed OAuth providers

Fixes #8257 (OAuth half ŌĆö email/send 400 remains Steward service)

**Note:** Supersedes #8355 (Feed PKCE folded into this PR).

topPRs.title: fix(feed): Steward OAuth PKCE for Feed login (#8257)
topPRs.author: dutchiono
topPRs.body:
## Summary
- Completes the **#8257 OAuth half** for the Feed app ŌĆö the only remaining monorepo login surface still building plain `/authorize` URLs without PKCE.
- Feed `LoginModal` now mints PKCE verifier/challenge, sends `code_challenge` + `response_type=code` at `/authorize`, and stores the verifier for callback replay.
- New `POST /api/auth/steward/oauth/exchange` forwards the code + verifier to Steward server-side (with optional `STEWARD_REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET` signing for prod).
- OAuth callback page handles `?code=` (PKCE flow) and keeps legacy `?token=` backward compat during rollout.

Related: #8350 covers cloud-frontend + os-homepage; this PR covers Feed.

## Test plan
- [x] `bun x vitest run apps/web/src/lib/steward-oauth.test.ts` (3 tests)
- [ ] Manual: Feed login ŌåÆ Google/Discord/X ŌåÆ callback completes sign-in
- [ ] Fork CI after maintainer workflow approval

Fixes #8257 (OAuth half ŌĆö email/send 400 remains Steward service)

topPRs.title: feat(apps): tenant-DB teardown ŌĆö deprovision + release slot on cleanup
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
Closes the **isolated-DB leak** half of #8342.

## Problem
Deleting an app with its own per-tenant Postgres **never DROPped** the DATABASE/ROLE and **never released** the cluster slot ŌĆö `SqlTenantDbProvisioner.deprovision` had zero callers, there was no `releaseSlot` (only `tryClaimSlot` incremented), and `cleanupDatabase` only handled the legacy Neon path. So every deleted isolated app stranded a live DB we keep paying for **and** burned one of the cluster's 2000 finite slots permanently.

## Fix
- **`releaseSlot(clusterId)`** ŌĆö the counterpart to `tryClaimSlot`; `GREATEST(0, count-1)` so a double-release can't drive the count negative. Plus **`findByHost(host)`** to resolve the owning cluster from the host in the app's stored DSN.
- **`deprovisionTenantDbForApp`** (pure, DI'd) ŌĆö parse the DSN host ŌåÆ resolve the cluster ŌåÆ DROP the DB+role ŌåÆ **then** release the slot (order matters: a failed DROP leaves the slot counted, so we never free a slot for a still-live DB). Fail-safe no-ops for a shared/unknown DSN.
- **`SqlTenantDbProvisioning.deprovisionForApp`** wires the use-case over the repo + admin executor; `make-tenant-db-provisioning` injects the real backends.
- **`UserDatabaseService.cleanupDatabase`** now tears down the isolated path too (was Neon-only), gated on the provisioning backend being wired (the daemon ŌĆö `DROP` needs `pg`); Worker-side it's a no-op.

## Tests
DSN-host parsing, drop-then-release ordering, fail-safe no-ops (no host / unknown cluster), a failed-DROP-keeps-the-slot case, the composer path, and the not-configured guard. **31 pass / 0 fail**; typecheck + biome clean.

## Scope / remaining
Additive, our tenant-DB lane only (no agent path, no 2AM billing files). The deprovision fires when `cleanupDatabase` runs on the **daemon**; wiring app-delete to trigger a daemon-side cleanup (an enqueue) is the final integration step ŌĆö gated on the live daemon, like the rest of the apps lane. Pairs with #8338 (opt-in isolated DB).

topPRs.title: feat(apps): make the per-tenant database opt-in (mode none|isolated)
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## What
The deploy orchestrator provisioned an isolated per-tenant Postgres for **every** app and injected `DATABASE_URL`/`POSTGRES_URL` unconditionally. That forces a database (and a tenant-DB cluster) on apps that don't need one ŌĆö e.g. a stateless SPA + stateless API like elocute.

Make the database a **per-app choice**, addable later, with no migration:

- **`apps.metadata.databaseMode`** (jsonb): `"none"` (default) or `"isolated"`.
  - **none** ŌåÆ stateless: no DB provisioned, **no** `DATABASE_URL`.
  - **isolated** ŌåÆ the app's OWN per-tenant Postgres, injected under both `DATABASE_URL` + `POSTGRES_URL`.
- `deployApp` reads the mode and only calls `ensureTenantDb` + injects the DSN when `isolated`. `DefaultAppDeployRunner` resolves the mode from the app.
- **Add a DB later ŌĆö seamless + lossless:** `PUT /api/v1/apps/:id/database {mode:"isolated"}` then redeploy. `ensureTenantDb` is create-if-not-exists, so the DB materializes and wires in. Flipping back to `none` stops injecting the DSN but **leaves the database intact** (destroying tenant data is a separate explicit action). `GET` returns the current mode + binding.

The endpoint is **Worker-safe** (only writes the metadata flag; provisioning happens on the next deploy on the daemon). The load-bearing invariant is preserved: an isolated app gets its **own** DSN, never the shared agent URL, never a silent PGlite fallback.

## Why
- elocute (the dogfood) is stateless ŌĆö it shouldn't pay for or require a tenant-DB node.
- Lets the platform host apps **with and without** a database, and lets an app **gain a database later** without a recreate.

## Tests / verification
- `app-database-mode.test.ts`: resolver defaults garbage/missing ŌåÆ `none`; reads explicit modes.
- `app-deploy-orchestrator.test.ts`: **isolated** ŌåÆ `ensureTenantDb` called + both DSN vars set; **none** (and default) ŌåÆ no provision, no `DATABASE_URL`, deploy still succeeds.
- **9/9 pass**, 100% line coverage on the new + changed service files; `typecheck` + `biome` clean; route codegen regenerated.

## Scope
Additive, our apps lane only (deploy orchestrator/runner + a new metadata helper + a new `/apps/:id/database` route). No schema migration, no 2AM hot files, no agent path.

completedItems.title: feat(infra): make the dedicated tenant-DB node optional (default off)
completedItems.body:
## Why
A dedicated ccx33 tenant-DB node (~$53/mo) is **pure unrecovered fixed cost at low density** ŌĆö the apps lane runs *negative* below ~3 paying containers (#8342). For beta we want **app-node-only** and co-locate the first few tenant DB

Bridges the **already-existing** custom-domain stack into the Product-2 Caddy apps ingress (#8325), so a user's own verified domain (e.g. `elocute.fun`) actually **serves their app** ŌĆö not just CORS + a DNS target.

After #8353 made `CONTAINERS_AUTOSCALE_MIN_FREE_SLOTS_BUFFER` env-configurable, staging was overridden to `2` to collapse to 1 node when idle, but prod kept the code default `4`. Same daemon code, different scaling shape ŌĆö silent en

completedItems.body:
Hardcoded buffer=4 forces 2 nodes minimum when any agent is allocated on a 4-capacity node. Right for prod, wasteful for staging.

completedItems.title: fix(infra): add name to ignore_changes for apps-data-plane VMs
completedItems.body: Drift guard, matches control-plane convention. Lets operator rename via Hetzner Console without next TF plan wanting to revert.

completedItems.title: fix(autoscaler): emit environment + tier labels on provision
completedItems.body:
## Summary

Hetzner servers provisioned by the node autoscaler only carried `managed-by` + `node-id`, so every API-discovered node looked identical across environments. A staging node ended up tagged `environment=production` from a hand-fix
